insight: Color permission checks only run for types listed in Colors.Apply_On, which defaults to [] in settings.yml, so MiniMessage tags typed in chat are escaped to literal plain text and LuckPerms verbose shows no chatcontrol.color.* check at all. To strip unauthorized tags from the message (like &#RRGGBB codes are), set Colors.Apply_On: [chat].

On builds before 12.2.0, escaped MiniMessage tags typed by players were revived as real colors in console, spy and proxy formats because the channel pipeline flattened the message to legacy and dropped the escape (fixed by escapeMiniMessageTags in Channel.java). If a user reports gradient tags rendering despite denied permissions, especially across Velocity/Bungee, have them update all backends to 12.2.0+ and VelocityControl to 3.1.0+.
